solution:  
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    int smallestRepunitDivByK(int k) {
        if (k % 2 == 0 || k % 5 == 0) return -1;

        int remainder = 0;
        for (int length = 1; length <= k; ++length) {
            remainder = (remainder * 10 + 1) % k;
            if (remainder == 0) return length;
        }
        return -1;
    }
};
```

efficiency: HIGH  
time complexity: O(k)  
space complexity: O(1)  
energy implications: LOW  
explanation: The solution leverages modular arithmetic to avoid constructing large numbers directly. It iteratively builds the remainder of numbers composed of only 1s modulo k. If the remainder becomes zero, the current length is returned. If no such number is found within k iterations, it returns -1. The key insight is that if no solution exists within k steps, it will never exist.
